name: La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail
name_original: Cueva de la Galera y pico de la Ratlla — circular desde Favara
description: Hike the difficult 5.9-mile La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail, gaining 1902 feet to Cova de la Galera and La Ratlla summit.
region_intro: Hiking around Favara offers diverse landscapes in the hills near Agrigento, Sicily. The region features picturesque valleys and mountains, with trails winding through lush forests, including undisturbed oaks and ancient cork trees in the Favara and Granza Forest Oriented Nature Reserve. Hikers can explore areas characterized by Mediterranean flora, agricultural landscapes with vineyards and olive groves, and significant peaks like Monte Roccellitto. This varied terrain provides a compelling mix of natural beauty for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Favara The most popular hiking route is La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail, a difficult 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views from the peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cova de la Galera loop from Favara, a difficult 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail explores the natural cave of Cova de la Galera and the surrounding hilly terrain. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Favara, a 3.9 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through varied local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Hiking around Favara is defined by its valleys, mountains, and dense forests, alongside agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Favara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Favara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Favara?
A: Favara offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 200 routes to explore. These trails traverse picturesque valleys, mountains, and dense forests, providing diverse landscapes for all outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Favara?
A: Hikers in Favara will encounter a rich tapestry of landscapes. Trails often wind through lush forests, including undisturbed oaks and ancient cork trees in the Favara and Granza Forest Oriented Nature Reserve. You'll also find Mediterranean flora, agricultural areas with vineyards and olive groves, and scenic valleys leading to significant peaks like Monte Roccellitto.

Q: Are there trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Favara caters to various fitness levels. Among the over 200 available routes, you'll find 57 easy trails perfect for a relaxed stroll. These routes allow you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant physical exertion.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Favara?
A: Absolutely. Many hikers prefer circular routes for convenience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14674536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Favara&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 6.4 km trail that takes you through varied local landscapes, offering a complete experience without retracing your steps.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see on a hike?
A: Favara's natural surroundings are rich with points of interest. You can explore impressive geological features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3297906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Galera&lt;/a&gt;, a natural cave located in the Sierra de Corbera. The Favara and Granza Forest also features the small, temporary Lake Bomes, which fills with autumn rains and adds a unique ecological element to the landscape.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The forests around Favara are home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for various birds such as the Sicilian long-tailed tit, tits, blackbirds, nightingales, hoopoes, and jays. Mammals like foxes, porcupines, and martens also inhabit the region's dense vegetation.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Favara offers plenty for experienced hikers. There are 33 difficult routes, including trails with significant elevation gain and rugged terrain. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375930610/la-ratlla-peak-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.6 km route that provides panoramic views from the peak.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Favara?
A: The hiking trails in Favara are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from lush forests to scenic valleys, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to interesting caves?
A: Yes, the region is known for its caves. You can hike to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3297906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Galera&lt;/a&gt;. A specific route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21936348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Galera loop from Favara&lt;/a&gt;, is a challenging 7.7 km path that explores this natural wonder and its surrounding hilly terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Favara?
A: Favara offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. The region's Mediterranean climate means spring and autumn are ideal, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Even in late spring, you can enjoy the trails, though some temporary ponds like Lake Bomes might dry up.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the surrounding mountains?
A: Many trails in Favara provide stunning mountain vistas. The region is characterized by picturesque valleys and mountains, with trails often leading to panoramic viewpoints. Peaks like Monte Roccellitto (1,145 m), Cozzo Bomes (1,073 m), and Pizzo Conca (1,002 m) offer excellent opportunities for breathtaking views.

Q: Can I explore other natural parks or reserves nearby?
A: Beyond the immediate Favara area, you can explore other significant natural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4758759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Municipal Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is another nearby option that offers beautiful natural landscapes and hiking opportunities.
tips: The spectacular natural cave of Ribera Baixa.

Top of the Serra de Corbera at 625 m asl with spectacular views towards the Levantine coast, Cullera, Tavernes and towards the other side the Serra de les Agulles and Vall de La Casella.
It can be reached by path with moderate technical difficulty, with sectors of loose stone and some steep steps.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular hike is estimated to take approximately 4 hours to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail is classified as difficult. It involves significant elevation gain and descent, making it suitable for experienced hikers with good physical conditioning.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable features. You'll pass by the intriguing Cova de la Galera and reach the impressive La Ratlla summit, which provides rewarding views.

faqs.question: Is the La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella, dogs are generally permitted. However, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the trail's difficult terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to enter the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella. However, always check local regulations before your visit, as policies can change.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for hiking the La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning starts essential, while winter might present colder conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As a circular trail, you can typically start and end at the same point. While specific parking areas aren't widely documented, look for designated parking near the starting point in Favara or consult local maps for public parking options close to the Paratge Natural Municipal La Casella.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Pic de la Ratlla trail and the Senda dels Pastors during your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the La Ratlla Peak Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and ascent to a peak, expect varied and challenging terrain. This likely includes rocky paths, steep ascents and descents, and potentially uneven surfaces. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Favara, a town that may offer some basic amenities like cafes or small shops. However, it's always best to pack all necessary supplies, including water and snacks, as facilities directly at the trailhead or along the route are limited.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this difficult trail?
faqs.answer: For a difficult hike like this, essential items include plenty of water, high-energy snacks, sturdy hiking boots, appropriate layered clothing, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), a first-aid kit, and a charged phone. A physical map or a downloaded offline map on your komoot app is also advisable.
